title: Wrong results in scalar subquery with double parenthesis
description:
### What happens?

Wrong results in scalar subquery with double parenthesis 


### To Reproduce

```
create table tenk1(
 unique1 int4,
 unique2 int4,
 two int4,
 four int4,
 ten int4,
 twenty int4,
 hundred int4,
 thousand int4);

insert into tenk1 values (1,1,1,1,1,1,1,1);
insert into tenk1 values (2,2,2,2,2,2,2,2);

select                              
  (select max((select i.unique2 from tenk1 i where i.unique1 = o.unique1)))
from tenk1 o;
```

Duckdb result : 

```
┌───────┐
│   x   │
│ int32 │
├───────┤
│     1 │
│     2 │
└───────┘
```

PG result : 

```
 max 
-----
   2
(1 row)
```



### OS:

aarch64

### DuckDB Version:

1.4.2

### DuckDB Client:

cli

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Zhibai Song

### Affiliation:

Aws

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, value VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW());
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value, created_at) VALUES (1, 'test1', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 00:00:00');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value, created_at) VALUES (2, 'test2', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-02 00:00:00');
SELECT (SELECT MAX(value) FROM test_table WHERE id = t.id) FROM test_table t;
SELECT COUNT(*) AS null_count FROM test_table WHERE value IS NULL;
key_bug_features: Scalar subquery with double parenthesis returns wrong results
